headline: Middle-income homebuyers have $30,000 more buying power than a year ago, research finds. It's still not enough
summary: While the amount that a median-income household can afford is higher than it was a year ago, it is still below the median price for a single-family home.

raw.headline: Middle-income homebuyers have $30,000 more buying power than a year ago, research finds. It's still not enough

headline: Lennar Voting Proposals Put Governance And Investor Influence In Focus
summary: Several shareholder proposals targeting voting rights and transparency at Lennar (NYSE:LEN) are on the agenda for the upcoming annual meeting. Key items include calls to grant equal voting rights to all shares and to disclose voting results by share class. Lennar has recommended voting against these measures, while some institutional investors are engaging on governance concerns. Lennar is a major U.S. homebuilder, so any shift in how shareholder votes are counted or reported could matter...

headline: Oracle earnings, February CPI, housing data: What to Watch
summary: Market Domination Overtime Host Jared Blikre previews several of the biggest stories to come throughout next week, including earnings results from Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), Kohl's (KSS), Oracle (ORCL), and Adobe (ADBE); February's Consumer Price Index survey (CPI) results; and the latest readings on NFIB Optimism Index and US existing home sales. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.
